paragraphs: Adverbial Clause 状语从句
Definition

◆ The clauses used as adverbial of the sentence are called adverbial clauses. They are used to show relationships between ideas.
◆ The association words that guide the adverbial clause are some subordinate conjunctions (从属连词). They are used to modify verbs, adjectives or adverbs.
◆ Adverbial clauses can be divided according to their usage into the adverbial clause of:
(A) The adverbial clause of time 时间状语从句
when 用法补充笔记

We use ‘when’ to talk about periods of our lives or periods of time past.
E.g. His mother called him Robbie when he was a baby.

We use ‘when’ to mean ‘every time’.
E.g. I still feel tired when I wake up in the morning.

When we want to say that when one thing changes, another thing changes at the same time. We prefer to use ‘as’.
E.g. As the day went on, the weather got worse.
(B) The adverbial clause of manner 方式状语从句
(Just) as ..., so ...

Just as the body needs regular exercise, so an engine needs to be run at regular intervals.
Just as French people enjoy their wine, so the British enjoy their beer.
as

I have changed it as you suggest.
Everything happened exactly as (it had been expected).
Most plants need air just as they need water.
(in) the way (that)

She’s doing her work the way I like it done.
Things developed just the way we had thought they would.
Form 5 English Usage Book p.18 of 138
as if /as though 仿佛，就像…… 一样
Subjunctive mood（虚拟语气）
The old man runs very fast as if he were a young man.
He behaved as if nothing had happened.
Stating facts（陈述事实，时态与主句保持一致）
The milk smells as if it is sour.
His mouth breath is so strong that he seems as if he hasn’t brushed for days. (to appear to sb . like)

◆ To show comparison: led by “as” or “than”
The clauses do not modify verbs but usually the adverbs “as, so, less, more” or the other adjectives or adverbs.
E.g. He earns less than his wife (does).
E.g. She knows more than I did at her age.
E.g. He is as tall as his father (is).
(C) The adverbial clause of reason 原因状语从句
(D) The adverbial clause of place 地点状语从句

The adverbial clause of place led by “where”, with the meaning “在（或到）…… 的地方”
E.g. Put it where you found it.

In the adverbial clause of place, “where” refers to not only the location, but can also refer to the situation, etc.
E.g. He said he was happy where he was. 他说他对自己的境况很满意。

The adverbial clause of place led by “wherever” with the meaning “在（或到）…… 的各个地方”
E.g. You can go wherever (anywhere) you like these days.
(E) The adverbial clause of result 结果状语从句
(F) The adverbial clause of purpose 目的状语从句
Result: , So (that) VS Purpose: So (that)

◆ USE the comma when it begins with a clause of result or effect.
E.g. It is difficult to relax during the week, so (that) I like to get away on weekends.
◆ DON’T USE the comma when you use ‘so that’ to explain why something happened in the clause of purpose;
E.g. We asked for approval early so (that) we could start on Monday.
E.g. The city wanted to tear down the building so that the area could become a park.
in case /for fear (that) /lest 生怕、唯恐
(G) The adverbial clause of concession 让步状语从句
(H) The adverbial clause of condition 条件状语从句

补充条件连词：On condition that; suppose/supposing that; assuming that
手写笔记：if so 如果（前文）那样
例：It might rain. If so, cancel.
tables: After she had graduated, she got a job.
I had left before he came.

When I arrived, he was talking on the phone.
When I was in Chicago, I visited the museums.
when = at that time / just then / one action after the other
While/As I was walking home, it began to rain.
By the time he arrived, we had already left.
By the time = one event is completed before another event

I haven’t seen him since he left this morning.
I’ve known her ever since I was a child.

since = from that time to the present
+ ever adds emphasis
We stayed there until / till we finished our work.

until/ till = to that time and then no longer
till (less formal)
as soon as / once / directly / the instant / the moment

As soon as it stops raining, we will leave.
Once it stops raining, we will leave.
Directly the teacher came in, everyone was quiet.
as soon as / once = when one event happens, another event happens soon afterward
Whenever/ Every time I see her, I say hello.
whenever = every time（repeated events 反复发生的动作）
the first time/ the last time/ the next time

The first time (that) I went to New York, I went to an opera.
I saw two plays the last time (that) I went to New York.
The next time (that) I go to New York, I’m going to see a ballet.

He had hardly arrived home when he was asked to start on another journey.
No sooner had the words been spoken than he realized that he should have remained silent.
Just at that moment; only in a very short time
used to show the connection between two situations /actions，语气比 as 更强

I didn’t go abroad with her because I couldn’t afford it.
Don’t scamp your work because you are pressed for time.

Direct cause / reason people do not know
Commonly used to answer to the interrogative led by “why”

Since Monday is a holiday, we don’t have to go to work.
Since you’re a good cook and I’m not, you should cook the dinner.

When since is used to mean “because”, it expresses a known cause.
It means “because it is a fact that” / “given that it is true that”

As I didn’t know the way, I asked a policeman.
As it is snowing, we shall not climb the mountain.

Send the known information
The clause is usually located before the main sentence
It can be replaced with “since”.
Suddenly it began to rain heavily, so (that) it was almost impossible to carry on driving.
Can only be used after the main sentence, WITH a comma in the middle.

so + adj/adv + that
such + a/an + N + that

She spoke so fast that nobody could catch what she was saying.
There is so little time left that I have to tell you about it later.
You are such a humorous person that people around you are always happy.

如此…… 以至于
“that” can be omitted in informal style, with adjectives or adverbs followed by “so”.
For adverbial clause of result, we can use modal verbs ‘must’, ‘can’, ‘could’

His progress was such that it surprised his teacher.
The job is such that it demands the greatest skill.

Tony studied hard so that/ in order that he would get an A in English.
OR
Tony studied hard so he would get an A in English.

Can only be used after the main sentence, WITHOUT a comma in the middle
Modal verbs ‘will’, ‘may’, ‘can’, ‘could’, ‘might’, ‘would’ are used in adverbial clause of purpose
You’ll need a good map, a compass, a cell phone for help in case you get lost.
They indicate negative purpose, which means “to avoid”, “prevent”.
Take your umbrella with you lest it (should) rain.

Can be used in subjunctive mood.
lest + (should) do
He took an umbrella with him for fear that it might rain.
The clause guided by “for fear (that)” is always used with modal verbs may, might, would, or should (subjunctive).
Although/ Even though/ though they have been talking for a long time, he cannot make her believe him.

I’m going to go swimming tomorrow even if it is cold.
Due to economic depression, even if you have a degree, you may not be able to find a good job.
Neither this nor that matters to the result; the result will be the same.（即使，不管怎样）
The house isn’t nice, I like the garden though.
In spoken English, use ‘though’ at the end of a sentence.
Much as I admire his courage, I don’t think he acted wisely.

While I understand your point of view, I do not agree with you.
The phone rang while I was studying.

It shows direct contrast: “this” is exactly the opposite of “that”.
“While” can also mean “during that time”.

We are determined to fulfill the task, whatever/no matter what happens.
However hard you try, you cannot achieve it.
= no matter what/ which/ who/ where/ how…
If I don’t eat breakfast, I get hungry.
A certain result will be produced.
You’ll be late unless you hurry.

I’ll let you use the car only if you keep it in good condition.
Only if it rains will the picnic be canceled.

There is only one condition that will cause a particular result.
When it begins a sentence, the subject and verb in the main clause are inverted.（放句首主句倒装）

If only I had enough money, I could buy that dictionary.
If only I had known it, I wouldn’t have troubled him.
Usually used in subjunctive mood（要是…… 就好了）
As long as/ So long as
As long as it doesn’t rain, we can play.
Attention: use present simple in the clause to refer to future time.（只要）
I will agree to go provided/ providing (that) my expense is paid.

Consult your teachers in case you have problems.
In case it rains, do not expect me.

假使
对比目的用法：
(purpose) Take your umbrella in case it rains.（带雨伞以防下雨）

paragraphs: ### 1. What you eat ______ a lot on your health.
* **Answer:** **A. affects**
* **Options:**
* **A. affects:** 影响 (动词，一般现在时单三)
* **B. effects:** 效果/影响 (名词复数)
* **C. effect:** 效果/影响 (名词单数)
* **D. infects:** 传染/感染 (动词单三)
### 2. A mother always ______ her children first no matter under what kind of circumstances.
* **Answer:** **A. protects**
* **Options:**
* **A. protects:** 保护 (动词单三)
* **B. protests:** 抗议 (动词单三)
* **C. contests:** 争辩/比赛 (动词单三)
* **D. protect:** 保护 (动词原形)
### 3. Jenny ______ the idea of having Mexican food tonight.
* **Answer:** **A. proposed**
* **Options:**
* **A. proposed:** 提议/建议 (动词过去式)
* **B. supposed:** 猜想/假定 (动词过去式)
* **C. reposed:** 休息/寄托 (动词过去式)
* **D. imposed:** 强加 (动词过去式)
### 4. When you blow air into the balloon, it could ______ to five times bigger or more.
* **Answer:** **A. expand**
* **Options:**
* **A. expand:** 膨胀/扩大 (动词)
* **B. expend:** 花费/消耗 (动词)
* **C. extend:** 延伸/延长 (动词)
* **D. extent:** 程度/范围 (名词)
### 5. It is not so difficult to ______ to the new job if you are fully prepared.
* **Answer:** **A. adapt**
* **Options:**
* **A. adapt:** 适应/改写 (动词，常与 to 连用)
* **B. adopt:** 收养/采用 (动词)
* **C. adept:** 熟练的/精通的 (形容词)
* **D. be adopted:** 被收养/被采用 (被动语态)
### 6. Please ______ your ticket again before going abroad.
* **Answer:** **A. confirm**
* **Options:**
* **A. confirm:** 确认/证实 (动词)
* **B. conform:** 遵守/符合 (动词，常与 to 连用)
* **C. reform:** 改革/改造 (动词)
* **D. firm:** 稳固的 (形容词) / 公司 (名词)
### 7. We are ______ to provide ID before going into the library.
* **Answer:** **C. required**
* **Options:**
* **A. requiring:** 要求 (现在分词)
* **B. inquired:** 询问/打听 (过去式/过去分词)
* **C. required:** 被要求 (过去分词，be required to 意为“被要求做某事”)
* **D. acquired:** 获得/学到 (过去式/过去分词)
### 8. It will be fair if we could look at this news from all the ______.
* **Answer:** **B. aspects**
* **Options:**
* **A. inpects:** *(此处为拼写错误，原词应为 inspects 视察/检查)*
* **B. aspects:** 方面/角度 (名词复数)
* **C. suspects:** 嫌疑犯 (名词) / 怀疑 (动词)
* **D. respects:** 尊重/敬意 (名词复数)
### 9. I ______ any form of racial discrimination.
* **Answer:** **B. resent**
* **Options:**
* **A. recent:** 最近的 (形容词)
* **B. resent:** 怨恨/愤恨/厌恶 (动词)
* **C. assent:** 同意/赞成 (动词/名词)
* **D. ascent:** 上升/攀登 (名词)
### 10. Winnie’s knowledge of biology is ______.
* **Answer:** **A. extensive**
* **Options:**
* **A. extensive:** 广泛的/广博的 (形容词)
* **B. intensive:** 集中的/精深的 (形容词)
* **C. intense:** 剧烈的/强烈的 (形容词)
* **D. tense:** 紧张的 (形容词)
### 11. Adam struck a ______ on the piano.
* **Answer:** **B. chord**
* **Options:**
* **A. chore:** 日常杂务/琐事 (名词)
* **B. chord:** 和弦/音符 (名词，strike a chord 意为“弹奏出和弦/引起共鸣”)
* **C. choir:** 唱诗班/合唱团 (名词)
* **D. [Blank]:** *(选项为空白)*
## Part 2: Questions 12–23 (Page 124)
### 12. The couple planned ______ to Finland to seek for better education.
* **Answer:** **B. to immigrate** *(移入芬兰，且 plan 后面接 to do)*
* **Options:**
* **A. emigrate:** 移居国外/移出 (动词原形)
* **B. to immigrate:** 移民进入 (动词不定式)
* **C. immigrate:** 移民进入 (动词原形)
* **D. to emigrate:** 移居国外/移出 (动词不定式)
### 13. Although ______ factors may properly explain the motives behind the criminal action, the police cannot arrest them for this reason.
* **Answer:** **B. causal**
* **Options:**
* **A. casual:** 偶然的/随便的/非正式的 (形容词)
* **B. causal:** 因果关系的 (形容词)
* **C. causes:** 原因 (名词复数) / 导致 (动词单三)
* **D. cause:** 原因 (名词单数) / 导致 (动词原形)
### 14. You can understand what happened approximately by the ______ of this letter.
* **Answer:** **C. context**
* **Options:**
* **A. contend:** 竞争/主张 (动词)
* **B. content:** 内容/目录 (名词)
* **C. context:** 上下文/语境/背景 (名词)
* **D. contest:** 比赛/争夺 (名词/动词)
### 15. It’s an ______ behaviour to take something from someone without asking.
* **Answer:** **B. immoral**
* **Options:**
* **A. immortal:** 不朽的/神仙的 (形容词)
* **B. immoral:** 不道德的 (形容词)
* **C. mortal:** 凡人的/致命的 (形容词)
* **D. moral:** 道德的 (形容词)
### 16. The GDP of our country ______ by 0.3 percent last season.
* **Answer:** **A. declined**
* **Options:**
* **A. declined:** 下降/衰退 (动词过去式)
* **B. reclined:** 斜倚/靠 (动词过去式)
* **C. inclined:** 倾向于 (动词过去式)
* **D. recline:** 斜倚/靠 (动词原形)
### 17. The birds singing and the flowers in blossom are ______ the arrival of spring.
* **Answer:** **D. proclaiming**
* **Options:**
* **A. exclaiming:** 呼喊/惊叫 (现在分词)
* **B. claiming:** 声称/索取 (现在分词)
* **C. reclaiming:** 收回/开拓 (现在分词)
* **D. proclaiming:** 宣告/声明/显示 (现在分词)
### 18. The lava ______ from the volcano has caused a huge damage.
* **Answer:** **B. ejected**
* **Options:**
* **A. rejected:** 拒绝/排斥 (过去式/过去分词)
* **B. ejected:** 喷出/排出 (过去式/过去分词)
* **C. injected:** 注射 (过去式/过去分词)
* **D. inject:** 注射 (动词原形)
### 19. Nothing in the world would ______ me to harm anybody on purpose.
* **Answer:** **A. induce**
* **Options:**
* **A. induce:** 引诱/劝使 (动词，induce sb. to do 意为“引诱某人做某事”)
* **B. deduce:** 推论/演绎 (动词)
* **C. reduce:** 减少 (动词)
* **D. produce:** 生产/产生 (动词)
### 20. The pill had great ______ on killing the pain.
* **Answer:** **B. effects** *(此处作名词，表示“效果”)*
* **Options:**
* **A. affects:** 影响 (动词单三)
* **B. effects:** 效果/影响 (名词复数)
* **C. infects:** 感染 (动词单三)
* **D. affect:** 影响 (动词原形)
### 21. The crowd went onto the street and ______ for children’s right.
* **Answer:** **A. protested**
* **Options:**
* **A. protested:** 抗议/示威 (动词过去式)
* **B. protected:** 保护 (动词过去式)
* **C. contested:** 争取/争辩 (动词过去式)
* **D. contest:** 比赛/争夺 (动词原形)
### 22. The government ______ a heavy tax on luxury goods.
* **Answer:** **D. imposed**
* **Options:**
* **A. proposed:** 提议 (动词过去式)
* **B. supposed:** 猜想 (动词过去式)
* **C. reposed:** 休息 (动词过去式)
* **D. imposed:** 征收/强加 (动词过去式，impose... on... 意为“对……征税/强加”)
### 23. Please don’t ______ your energy on useless information.
* **Answer:** **B. expend**
* **Options:**
* **A. expand:** 膨胀/扩大 (动词)
* **B. expend:** 花费/消耗 (动词，expend energy on 意为“在……上消耗能量”)
* **C. extend:** 延伸/延长 (动词)
* **D. extent:** 程度 (名词)
## Part 3: Questions 24–36 (Page 125)
### 24. She’s very ______ at dealing with the media.
* **Answer:** **C. adept**
* **Options:**
* **A. adapt:** 适应 (动词)
* **B. adopt:** 采用/收养 (动词)
* **C. adept:** 熟练的/擅长的 (形容词，be adept at 意为“擅长于……”)
* **D. adapted:** 适应了的/改写的 (过去式/过去分词)
### 25. It is important to ______ the laws when driving.
* **Answer:** **B. conform**
* **Options:**
* **A. confirm:** 确认 (动词)
* **B. conform:** 遵守/符合 (动词，conform to the laws 意为“遵守法律”)
* **C. reform:** 改革 (动词)
* **D. firm:** 稳固的 (形容词)
### 26. Ariel had just ______ a big amount of scholarship from her university.
* **Answer:** **A. acquired**
* **Options:**
* **A. acquired:** 获得/取得 (过去分词)
* **B. required:** 需要/要求 (过去分词)
* **C. inquired:** 询问 (过去分词)
* **D. require:** 需要/要求 (动词原形)
### 27. Animals will usually run away if they ______ there’s danger.
* **Answer:** **C. suspect**
* **Options:**
* **A. aspect:** 方面 (名词)
* **B. inspect:** 检查/视察 (动词)
* **C. suspect:** 怀疑/察觉 (动词)
* **D. respect:** 尊重 (动词/名词)
### 28. I am sorry but I cannot ______ to such a request.
* **Answer:** **C. assent**
* **Options:**
* **A. recent:** 最近的 (形容词)
* **B. resent:** 怨恨 (动词)
* **C. assent:** 同意/赞成 (动词，assent to 意为“同意……”)
* **D. ascent:** 上升 (名词)
### 29. We plan to give the students ______ tests before the actual exam comes. It is reported that they are very ______.
* **Answer:** **A. intensive; tense**
* **Options:**
* **A. intensive; tense:** 集中的/高强度的；紧张的
* **B. extensive; intense:** 广泛的；剧烈的/强烈的
* **C. intense; intense:** 剧烈的；剧烈的
* **D. extensive; tense:** 广泛的；紧张的
### 30. A lot of mothers are excellent at both working and doing daily ______.
* **Answer:** **D. chores**
* **Options:**
* **A. chords:** 和弦 (名词复数)
* **B. choirs:** 合唱团 (名词复数)
* **C. choir:** 合唱团 (名词单数)
* **D. chores:** 家务杂事 (名词复数)
### 31. Millions of Germans ______ from Europe to America in the 19th century.
* **Answer:** **B. emigrated** *(从欧洲移出)*
* **Options:**
* **A. emigrate:** 移居国外/移出 (动词原形)
* **B. emigrated:** 移居国外/移出 (过去式)
* **C. immigrate:** 移民进入 (动词原形)
* **D. immigrated:** 移民进入 (过去式)
### 32. Just wear something ______ to our party; we don’t have a dress code.
* **Answer:** **B. casual**
* **Options:**
* **A. causal:** 因果关系的 (形容词)
* **B. casual:** 便装的/休闲的/随便的 (形容词)
* **C. causes:** 原因 (名词复数)
* **D. cause:** 原因 (名词单数)
### 33. To ______ for the first prize, you must put in all your efforts.
* **Answer:** **A. contend**
* **Options:**
* **A. contend:** 竞争/争夺 (动词，contend for 意为“争夺……”)
* **B. content:** 满足的 (形容词) / 内容 (名词)
* **C. context:** 上下文/语境 (名词)
* **D. contest:** 比赛/争夺 (动词/名词)
### 34. No one can live forever yet art and music are ______.
* **Answer:** **A. immortal**
* **Options:**
* **A. immortal:** 不朽的/永生的 (形容词)
* **B. immoral:** 不道德的 (形容词)
* **C. mortal:** 凡人的/终有一死的 (形容词)
* **D. moral:** 道德的 (形容词)
### 35. He ______ on the sofa in order to read the newspaper comfortably.
* **Answer:** **B. reclined**
* **Options:**
* **A. declined:** 拒绝/下降 (过去式)
* **B. reclined:** 斜靠/斜躺 (过去式)
* **C. inclined:** 倾向于 (过去式)
* **D. recline:** 斜靠/斜躺 (动词原形)
### 36. The children ______ with excitement when they finished their exams.
* **Answer:** **A. exclaimed**
* **Options:**
* **A. exclaimed:** 呼喊/大声叫嚷 (过去式)
* **B. claimed:** 声称/要求 (过去式)
* **C. reclaimed:** 收回/开拓 (过去式)
* **D. proclaimed:** 宣布/公告 (过去式)
## Part 4: Questions 37–38 (Page 126)
### 37. The girl ______ the boy’s invitation to the ball.
* **Answer:** **A. rejected**
* **Options:**
* **A. rejected:** 拒绝 (过去式)
* **B. ejected:** 喷出/驱逐 (过去式)
* **C. injected:** 注射 (过去式)
* **D. inject:** 注射 (动词原形)
### 38. We cannot ______ very much from these figures. We need further information to support the theory.
* **Answer:** **B. deduce**
* **Options:**
* **A. induce:** 引导/引诱 (动词)
* **B. deduce:** 推论/推断 (动词)
* **C. reduce:** 减少 (动词)
* **D. produce:** 生产/产生 (动词)
選項單詞詞彙表：
| 單詞 | 詞性 | 中文意思 |
| affect | v. | 影響 |
| effect | n. | 效果；影響 |
| infect | v. | 感染；傳染 |
| protect | v. | 保護 |
| protest | v./n. | 抗議；示威 |
| contest | v./n. | 競爭；爭奪；比賽 |
| propose | v. | 提議；建議 |
| suppose | v. | 猜想；假定 |
| repose | v. | 休息；寄託 |
| impose | v. | 強加；徵收 |

paragraphs: Participles 分词
1. Participles may occur in these forms:
2. Participles can be used:
A. As adjectives.

E.g. Mrs. Chan is an amusing person.
I like fried chicken legs.
Notes:

a. When the participle qualifying a noun is a phrase, it is put after the noun it qualifies.
E.g. The woman arguing with the hawker is Mrs. Tam.
The woman knocked down by a lorry died in hospital this morning.

b. To decide whether to use the present or the past participle to describe a noun, you must remember:
① that the present participle refers to a continuous action whereas the past participle refers to a completed action.
E.g. Look! People are running out the burning building.
It broke Mr. Chan’s heart to see his burnt hut.

② that the present participle is active in meaning and that the past participle is passive.
E.g. More laboratory facilities are needed to find out the amount of synthetic hormones contained in frozen meat.

E.g. The film was boring. It makes us feel bored.
(= the film caused boredom; we received the feeling)
The man is amusing. He always does funny things.
(= the man offers amusement; we receive the amusement)
B. In adverbials expressing...
◆ Time
◆ Reason
◆ Condition
◆ Concession
◆ Manner
Notes:
In using participle phrases as adverbials, pay attention to the following points:

a. The negative is formed by adding not before the participle phrase.
She could not go with us because she had not completely recovered from her illness.
→ Not having completely recovered from her illness, she could not go with us.

b. If the subject of the adverb clause which is to be replaced by the participle phrase is not the same person or thing as the subject of the main clause, it cannot be omitted.
As it was a cold day, Mary put on her coat.
→ It being a cold day, Mary put on her coat.

Compare:
As Mary felt cold, she put on her coat.
→ Feeling cold, Mary put on her coat.

c. When the verb in the clause is in the continuous tense, we may use the construction when/while + present participle.
Be careful when you are crossing the street.
→ Be careful when crossing the street.

d. Sometimes we can use the on + gerund construction to replace a participle phrase expressing time.
As soon as she heard the news, she collapsed.
Hearing the news, she collapsed.
On hearing the news, she collapsed.

e. Care should be taken that the logical subject of the action expressed by the participle in the phrase should be the same person or thing as the subject of the main clause.
Reading the newspaper, I was shocked by the news of the brutal murder.

However, there are cases where a participle phrase may not be logically related to the subject of the main clause. This occurs in a number of expressions so frequently used that they have been accepted as correct.
Here are some examples:
Generally speaking, a person’s experience is in proportion to his age.
Beginning tomorrow, tickets for the concert will be available.
There will be five of us going,
C. As object complement

The present participle can be used as an object complement placed after the object.
He found a letter lying on the floor.
He kept me waiting for three hours under the sun.

Being in a hurry, Tom took a taxi.

Although never having been there before, he found the house without any difficulty.

陪審團已經作出了裁決。
例句：He is the man who always renders good for evil.
他一直是個以德報怨的人。
precaution n. 預防，防備，警惕
safety precaution 安全措施 sanitary precaution 衛生預防 by way of precaution 為小心起見
例句：You should take an umbrella just as a precaution.
你該帶把傘，有備無患。
idle a. 懶散的，無所事事的
例句：A group of idle urchins had taken possession of the door-steps.
門前臺階已為一群沒事做的玩童所霸佔。
例句：To be idle sometimes is agreeable, but to be idle all the time might become monotonous.
一段時間空閒是令人愉快的，整日無所事事可能就單調乏味了。
identify vt. 認出，鑒定
例句：Please identify the registered trademark.
請認明注冊商標。
例句：The test can identify the presence of abnormalities in the unborn child.
這項化驗能鑒定胎兒是否有不正常現象。
poverty n. 貧窮
例句：Beware of the poverty gap trap.
小心貧富差距的陷阱。
resistant a. (to) 抵抗的，抗...的，耐...的
例句：A healthy diet creates a body resistant to disease.
保健飲食有助於增強體內對疾病的抵抗力。
resolve vt. 解決;決定，決意
例句：Intensive efforts are being made to resolve the dispute.
現正全力以赴來解決這場糾紛。
例句：He resolved that nothing should hold him back.
他下決心不讓任何事情阻擋他。
barrel n. 桶
例句：Beer swilled around the bottom of the barrel.
啤酒在桶底晃蕩。
bargain n. 便宜貨 vi. 討價還價
例句：Apples were on sale and the people snapped up the bargain.
蘋果在大減價，人們爭著購買。
例句：The salesman refused to bargain over the price.
推銷員拒絕討價還價。
coarse a. 粗的，粗糙的，粗劣的
詞義辨析：coarse, rough, harsh, rude, crude
coarse: 指某物質地低劣或編織、加工等不精細。也指(言談)粗俗。
rough: 普通用詞，指某物表面凹凸不平，天氣惡劣，(態度)粗暴，(計算)粗略。
harsh: 指外表、性質、聲音的粗糙、刺耳、刺目，強調使人不愉快感。
rude: 指加工粗糙、不精細。尤指行為舉止粗魯無禮，缺乏修養。
crude: 主要指未經加工處理尚處於自然狀態的物質;食物或水果的不熟。
例句：Homespun is a cloth of coarse weave.
土布是一種織得粗糙的布。
coach n. 教練;長途公共汽車
例句：The coach is bringing on some girls in the reserve team.
教練正教導預備隊的女孩們練球。
例句：The coach was full, so a relief was put on.
長途汽車已滿員，所以增開了一輛。
code n. 準則，法規，密碼
例句：Managers are expected to comply with the company's code of conduct.
希望經理能夠遵守公司的行為準則。
例句：The Highway Code has been carried out.
公路法規已經被執行。
例句：Deciphering the first code is the most difficult step.
破譯第一個密碼是最困難的一步。
coil n. 線圈 v. 卷，盤繞
例句：The birds are entangled in the coil of a net.
鳥被纏在網圈裡了。
例句：The snake coiled itself around the tree.
蛇盤繞在樹上。
adult n. 成年人
詞義辨析：adult, grown-up, mature
adult: 一般指生理上的童年期已結束，達到法定年齡。
grown-up: 多用於口語，含義與adult基本相同，但更側重脫離兒童階段，已成大人，與childish意思相反。
mature: 用於生物時，指完全長好了。用於人時，指達到了生命的黃金時期，但所暗示的青少年和成年之間的界限不明顯。
例句：These films are suitable for adults only.
這些電影只適宜成人觀看。
advertise v. 為...做廣告
advertise for 用廣告徵求
例句：It was time to advertise our cause instead of our cars.
當時的當務之急是我們的事業可不是為我們的汽車進行廣告宣傳。
advertisement n. 廣告
advertisement matter 廣告郵件 advertisement tax 廣告稅
例句：A lot of people replied to our advertisement.
有許多人對我們的廣告作出了反應。
agency n. 代理商，經銷商
Do you mean to say that if we entrust you with the agency, you will sell $1,000, 000 each year?
你的意思是說如果我們指定你們為代理，你將每年銷售100萬美元?
例句：I work in a tourist agency.
我在一家旅行社工作。
focus v. (使)聚集 n. 焦點，中心，聚焦
例句：He focused the sun's rays on the paper with a burning glass.
他用取火鏡使太陽光聚集在紙上。
例句：He always wants to be the focus of attention.
他總想成為注意力的焦點。
forbid vt. 不許，禁止
例句：Smoking is forbidden in this office.
這間辦公室禁止吸煙。
debate n. /v. 辯論，爭論
詞義辨析：debate, argue, quarrel, dispute
debate: 側重指意見等對立的雙方之間正式或公開的爭辯
argue: 指提出理由或證據為自己或自己一方的看法或立場辯護，著重說理、論證和企圖說服
quarrel: 指兩人之間或兩個團體之間不友好的、吵吵嚷嚷地大聲爭論某事，尤指“吵嘴、吵架”
dispute: 側重對分歧進行激烈或熱烈的爭論或爭辯，帶一定感情色彩，常隱含“各持已見”或“爭論不休”意味
例句：After a long debate the bill was passed.
經長時間的辯論，議案獲得通過。
debt n. 欠債
debt collector 收債人 bad debt 呆帳，壞賬，死帳 national debt 國債
例句：He promised to pay his debt within a month.
他保證在1個月內償還債務。
decade n. 十年
decade scaling 十進換算
例句：Prices have risen steadily during the past decade.
在過去十年裡，物價一直在上漲。
enclose vt. 圍住;把...裝入信封
enclose with 附上，將(某物)裝入(信封、包裹等中)
例句：Farmers often enclose their land with hedges.
農夫們常用樹籬將他們的地圍起來。
例句：I enclose herewith two copies of the contract.
隨函寄上合同副本兩份。
encounter vt. /n. 遭遇，遭到
詞義辨析：meet, encounter, confront, face, contact
meet: 普通用詞，本義指雙方或多方從不同方向或相反方向作相對運動，最終相碰(遇)。
encounter: 通常指遇到困難或挫折，也指偶然或意外地相遇。
confront: 不可避免地，面對面地相遇，也指敢於正視困難或問題。
face: 側重雙方靜止地面對面，或指充滿勇氣、信心和決心去正視人或事。
contact: 多指通過書信、電話或直接會面和別人聯繫，口語用詞。
例句：If both participate, it may be an actual encounter of bodiless consciousness.
如果是雙向的，這會是一個真實的與靈魂的相見。
例句：I encountered an old friend at Rome.
我在羅馬邂逅了一個老朋友。
globe n. 地球，世界;地球儀
例句：Magellan was the first person to circumnavigate the globe.
麥哲倫是第一個環球航行的人。
global a. 全球的;總的
global village 地球村，世界村 global warming 全球氣候變暖
例句：The green campaign is a global phenomenon.
綠色運動是全球性的現象。
scan vt. 細看;掃描;流覽
例句：I scanned my pocket for change.
我仔細查看囗袋找零錢。
例句：The doctors gave him an ultrasonic brain scan.
醫生給他做了腦部超聲波掃描檢查。
例句：I'm too busy to read the whole newspaper, so I just scan the headlines.
我太忙了，沒法看整份報紙，只是流覽了一下大標題。
scandal n. 醜事，醜聞
例句：A crime or scandal brings much notoriety to those involved in it.
罪行或醜聞常使有牽連的人聲名狼藉。
significance n. 意義;重要性
例句：He puzzled out the significance of the statement.
他解釋了這一論斷的意義。
例句：In modern anthropology, animism as a theory has little significance, other than historical.
在現代人類學中，泛靈論作為理論來說，除具有歷史意義外，已沒有多大意義。
subsequent a. 隨後的，後來的
subsequent to 在... 之後, 隨後
例句：The chairman will be pleased to discuss the matter with you subsequent to lunch.
主席很高興能與你在吃過午餐之後討論這個事件。
例句：Subsequent events proved me wrong.
後來發生的事證明我錯了。
virtue n. 美德，優點
例句：Fidelity to engagement is a virtue.
信守諾言是一種美德。
例句：Meekness is a virtue he appreciates in his wife.
溫順是他所欣賞的妻子的優點之一。
virtual a. 實際上的，事實上的
例句：A virtual state of war exists between the two countries.
這兩國間實際上處於戰爭狀態。
orient vt. 使適應，(to, toward)使朝向 n. 東方
例句：I need some time to orient my thinking.
我需要一些時間來使我的思想適應。
例句：They love paintings from the Orient.
他們喜歡來自東方的繪畫。
moisture n. 潮濕
例句：The plants absorb moisture from the soil.
植物從土壤中吸取水分。
promote vt. 促進;提升
例句：They discussed how to promote cooperation between the two countries.
他們討論如何促進兩國間的合作。
例句：He has been promoted to general manager.
他已被提升為總經理。
region n. 地區;範圍;幅度
例句：I guess there are much fewer people in the Arctic regions than here.
我想北極地區的人要比這裡的少得多。
例句：The region near the erupting volcano was evacuated rapidly.
火山爆發處附近已迅速撤空。
register v./n.登記，註冊
例句：You must register as a member first.
你必須先註冊成為會員。
例句：No register of his death was found.
沒有查到他的死亡記錄。
stable a. 穩定的
例句：Markets are flourishing and prices are stable.
市場繁榮, 物價穩定。
sophisticated a. 老於世故的，老練的;很複雜的
例句：She is such a sophisticated woman that she saw through his trick at the first sight.
她是個十分老練的女人,她一眼就看穿了他的把戲。
例句：The publisher's techniques for the promotion of his books have become increasingly sophisticated since the 1950s.
自20世紀50年代以來,出版商推銷書籍的方式愈加複雜。
splendid a. 極好的，壯麗的，輝煌的
例句：My kid sister has a splendid memory.
我小妹記憶力極好。
例句：The royal couple appeared in splendid array.
王室伉儷身穿盛裝出現。
例句：We won another splendid victory.
我們又贏得了輝煌的勝利。
英語四級高頻詞彙大全（六）
cancel vt. 取消，廢除
例句：The match had to be cancelled due to the bad weather.
比賽因天氣不好只得取消。
例句：I've lost my credit card, so I have to go to the bank to cancel it.
我的信用卡弄丟了,我得去銀行申請作廢。
variable a. 易變的，可變的
例句：Prices are variable according to the rate of exchange.
物價會隨匯率而變動。
例句：His mood is as variable as the weather.
他的情緒如天氣般反覆無常。
prospect n. 前景，前途;景象
例句：He has good prospects.
他大有前途。
例句：What do you think about the marketing prospect?
你覺得市場前景怎麼樣?
例句：The prospect from the balcony was breathtaking.
從陽臺上看去景色美極了。
prosperity n.興旺，繁榮
例句：The empire was on the wane after its period of prosperity.
帝國經過全盛以後逐漸衰落。
例句：Prosperity destroys fools and endangers the wise.
繁榮興旺既毀了蠢人，也使智者陷於險境。
aspect n. 方面;朝向;面貌
例句：This is only one aspect of the problem.
這只是問題的一個方面。
例句：The building has a southern aspect.
那座建築是朝南的。
例句：His brother is a man with a serious aspect.
他的兄弟是一個外貌嚴肅的人。
scope vi. (with)(成功地)應付，處理
例句：I don't know how she copes with looking after her family and doing a full-time job.
既要照顧家庭又要全天工作，我不知道她是如何對付的。
core n. 果心，核心
例句：He ate the plum, and threw the core away.
他吃掉了李子，並扔掉了果核。
例句：The core of our appeal is freedom of speech.
我們的要求的核心是言論自由。
maintain vt. 維持，保持;堅持，主張
例句：It is absolutely vital that food supplies should be maintained.
維持食物的供給是絕對必要的。
例句：It is clearly illogical to maintain such a proposition.
堅持這種主張顯然是沒有道理的。
例句：He maintains that there are no shortcuts to effective management.
他斷言,有效的管理沒有捷徑可走。
mainland n. 大陸, 本土
例句：The island is joined to the mainland by a bridge.
島上有座橋與大陸相連。
例句：Hawaii and Alaska are not on the mainland of the United States.
夏威夷和阿拉斯加不在美國本土。
discipline n. 紀律;懲罰;學科
例句：She is strict in the matter of discipline.
她對紀律的要求是嚴格的。
例句：A little discipline would do him a world of good.
稍微處分他一下會對他大有好處。
例句：Applied linguistics is a new and independent discipline.
應用語言學是一門新興的獨立學科。
domestic a. 本國的，國內的;家用的;家庭的
例句：Washington responded by freezing the price of domestic oil.
華盛頓做出的反應是凍結國內的石油價格。
例句：My value for domestic tranquility should much exceed theirs.
我應該遠比他們重視家庭的平靜生活。
constant a. 不變的，恒定的 n. 常數
例句：In our world nothing seems constant.
在我們這個世界上似乎沒有東西是永恆不變的。
例句：The speed of light is an important constant.
光速是一個重要的常數。
cliff n. 懸崖，峭壁
例句：The sea had undermined the cliff.
海水侵蝕了懸崖的基部。
authority n. 權威;當局
例句：We see him as an authority on the subject.
我們把他看作為這方面的權威。
例句：The new building must be financed by the local authority.
新大樓的建築資金必須由地方當局提供。
audio a. 聽覺
例句：Students is very interested in the audio-visual instruction.
學生們對視聽教學非常感興趣。
attitude n. 態度
attitude後常接towards/to，指“對於……的態度，看法”，如her attitude towards/to the question(她對這個問題的看法)。
值得注意的是，因上面例子中的賓語是question，有時候可以用on代替to，如her attitude on the question。
例句：She shows a very positive attitude to her work.
她工作態度非常積極。
community n. 社區，社會
例句：Everyone should invest some time in community service.
每個人都應該花些時間在社區服務上。
例句：The welfare of the community is the same as the welfare of its members.
社會的福利就等於其成員的福利。
commit vt. 犯(錯誤，罪行等)，幹(壞事等)
commit oneself to doing something 使自己不得免於……
例句：They committed outrages on innocent citizens.
他們對無辜的市民犯下了暴行。
comment n. /vt. 評論
例句：He made no comments on our proposal.
他對我們的建議沒有作評論。
例句：He commented that it was an excellent film.
他評論這電影很精彩。
distinguish vt. 區分，辨別
詞義辨析：distinguish, discern, discriminate
distinguish: 普通用詞，指辨別者的能力或實際觀察到的區別，強調辨別所需的技巧。
discern: 語義較籠統，著重精深與準確的觀察力，不一定有能力辨別關係密切的各項事物。
discriminate: 語氣強，指從十分相似的事物中辨出差異，尤其把好壞區分開來。
例句：Speeches distinguish human beings from animals.
人類和動物的區別在於人會說話。
distress n. 痛苦，悲傷 vt. 使痛苦
例句：I pray that you will have pity on my distress.
求你可憐可憐我的不幸遭遇。
例句：He couldn't distress her with his own terrible dilemma.
他不能用自己糟糕的困境來折磨她。
facility n. [pl.] 設備，設施;便利，方便
例句：The college has excellent sporting facilities.
這所學院有極好的體育設備。
例句：Certainly geothermometry will be used with greater facility in sub-soil exploration and in detecting migrant fluids.
可以肯定，地熱測量法在地下勘探和測試運移流體方面更便於使用。
faculty n. 能力，技能;系，學科，學院;全體教員
例句：This boy has a faculty of making friends easily.
這個男孩有交友的能力。
例句：He is the dean of the Faculty of Medicine.
他是醫學院院長。
例句：Both faculty and students oppose the measures.
全體教師和學生都反對這種方法。
mixture n. 混合，混合物
例句：You need a spoon to beat the mixture smooth.
你需要用一把調羹來攪均混合液。
例句：Air is a mixture, not a compound of gases.
空氣是氣體的混合物，不是化合物。
mood n. 心情，情緒;語氣
the subjunctive mood 虛擬語氣
例句：I'm not in the mood to disagree with you.
我沒有心思跟你爭論。
moral a. 道德上的，有道德的
例句：His behavior transgressed the moral rules of the social conduct.
他的行為違背了社會行為的道德準則。
prominent a. 突出的
例句：He is a prominent scholar in the field of linguistics.
他是語言學領域的傑出學者。
substance n. 物質;實質
例句：The emery is a hard metallic substance.
金剛砂是非常堅硬的金屬物質。
例句：There is not anything of real substance in their book.
他們的書中沒有什麼實質性的內容。
substantial a. 可觀的;牢固的;實質的
例句：Substantial profits accrue when sales take off.
當銷售額突飛猛進之後，可觀的利潤就開始自然累積。
例句：Those workers have built many substantial buildings in the recent years.
近幾年那些工人建造了許多堅固的建築。
例句：The reforms have made substantial headway.
改革取得了實質的進展。
prompt vt. 促使 a. 敏捷的，及時的
例句：Science and technology has prompted the rapid development of modern society.
科學技術推動了現代社會的迅速發展。
例句：Thank you for your prompt reply to our telegram.
多謝您對我們電報的及時答覆。
vivid a. 生動的
詞義辨析：vivid, lively, pictorial, picturesque
vivid: 普通用詞，側重有強烈的實際感或逼真感，使人富於想像或留下深刻的印象
lively: 普通用詞，與vivid可通用
pictorial: 強調產生或製造生動的效果
picturesque: 既指景色、人物等猶如圖畫般美麗，又可指文藝作品的風格絢麗多彩，尤指一種原始粗獷的美
例句：Dickens’ novels are full of vivid characterization.
狄更斯的小說中充滿了栩栩如生的人物形象。
vocabulary n. 詞彙(量);詞彙表
例句： If your vocabulary is limited your chances of success are limited.
如果你的詞彙量不大，你成功的機會也就不多。
venture n. 風險投資，風險專案v. 冒險;取於
例句：The hi-tech industry attracts much venture capital.
高科技產業吸引著許多風險投資。
例句：Don't venture into the jungle without a guide.
如果沒有嚮導,就不要冒險到森林裡去。
version n. 版本，譯本;說法
例句：The new version of this dictionary is not in use yet.
這本字典的新版本還未通行。
例句：The book was translated into many versions and sold all over the world.

### 備註
* ***** **consideration**（考慮）與 **considerable**（相當大的）屬於同一字根，但意思有所不同，考試常一起考。
* ****** **encourage** 並不是 **courage** 的動詞形式，而是由同一字根衍生出的常用動詞，意思是「鼓勵」。
* **casual**（隨便的）和 **casualty**（傷亡者）雖然字形相似，但不是同一個詞性的變化，不要混淆。
* **numerously、blazingly、praisingly** 等副詞在日常英文中很少使用，了解即可，不建議特別背。
